message:
fix(widgets/keyboard_drag): clear stale selection and harden drop path

Several related bugs in KeyboardDragManager around what happens when
the set of available drop targets changes mid-drag or the
announcement queue is aggressively tight.

1. navigate_targets() leaked stale selection on empty/invalid target
   sets.

   Before: if targets became empty, or none of them could accept the
   current drag type, navigate_targets() just returned None and left
   state.selected_target_index / state.mode untouched — so a later
   handle_key(Activate) would happily try to drop onto an index that
   no longer corresponded to any valid target.

   After: on every "no valid selection" early return
   (`targets.is_empty()`, `valid_indices.is_empty()`, `target_index
   >= targets.len()`, `!target.can_accept(...)`) the manager now
   resets selected_target_index = None and mode =
   KeyboardDragMode::Holding so the next key press has to re-navigate.

2. drop_on_target() was dereferencing a stale index.

   Before: the function took state unconditionally, then indexed
   `targets[target_idx]` with a plain `?`, which silently dropped the
   entire drag session if the index was out of range OR if the target
   at that index could no longer accept the payload.  That made it
   impossible for the caller to distinguish "user dropped onto
   nothing" from "drag ended".

   After: first validate target_idx against the current targets slice
   and can_accept() *without* consuming the state, announce an
   informative message ("Selected drop target unavailable" /
   "...no longer accepts this item"), reset the selection, and leave
   the drag active so the user can navigate again.  Only take() the
   state once we're committed to a successful drop.  A new
   clear_selected_target() helper centralizes the reset.

3. queue_announcement() priority queue was broken in two edge cases.

   a. When max_announcement_queue == 0 the old code still tried to
      push, so we'd accumulate announcements in an unbounded queue
      (fast path through `len() >= 0`). Now we early-return.

   b. When the queue was full, the old code unconditionally evicted
      the lowest-priority entry to make room for the new one.  That
      means a low-priority incoming announcement could kick out a
      *higher-priority* existing one.  Now we record both the
      position and the priority of the lowest entry, and bail out
      (dropping the incoming one) if it isn't strictly higher than
      the minimum already in the queue.

4. Tests:
   - manager_navigate_empty_targets now asserts the selection/mode
     reset.
   - New manager_navigate_no_valid_targets_clears_stale_selection
     exercises the type-mismatch branch and follows up with a
     handle_key(Activate) to prove the stale selection can't fire.
   - New manager_announcement_queue_zero_discards_announcements and
     manager_lower_priority_announcement_does_not_evict_higher_priority
     cover the queue fixes.
   - New manager_drop_on_invalidated_target_keeps_drag_active covers
     the drop_on_target hardening: drop fails, drag stays active,
     selection cleared, mode back to Holding.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
